papers.title: Unlocking Complex Visual Generation via Closed-Loop Verified Reasoning

primary_link.title: OpenDeepThink: Parallel Reasoning via Bradley--Terry Aggregation

papers.summary: Test-time compute scaling is a primary axis for improving LLM reasoning. Existing methods primarily scale depth by extending a single reasoning trace. Scaling breadth by sampling multiple candidates in parallel is straightforward, but introduces a selection bottleneck: choosing the best candidate without a ground-truth verifier, since pointwise LLM judging is noisy and biased. papers.summary_zh: 測試時運算擴展是改善 LLM 推理的主要途徑。現有方法主要透過擴展單一推理軌跡來擴展深度。透過並行採樣多個候選來擴展廣度是直接的，但會引入選擇瓶頸：在沒有 ground-truth 驗證器的情況下選擇最佳候選，因為逐點 LLM 判斷是嘈雜且有偏的。為了解決這個問題，我們引入 OpenDeepThink，這是一個基於群體（population-based）的測試時運算框架，它透過成對 Br
papers.title: OpenDeepThink: Parallel Reasoning via Bradley--Terry Aggregation

links.title: Case-Based Calibration of Adaptive Reasoning and Execution for LLM Tool Use

primary_link.title: Case-Based Calibration of Adaptive Reasoning and Execution for LLM Tool Use

papers.summary: Tool use extends large language models beyond parametric knowledge, but reliable execution requires balancing appropriate reasoning depth with strict structural validity. We approach this problem from a case-based perspective to present CAST, a case-driven framework that treats historical execution trajectories as structured cases. papers.summary_zh: 工具使用將大型語言模型擴展到參數知識之外，但可靠的執行需要平衡適當的推理深度與嚴格的結構有效性。我們從基於案例的角度處理這個問題，提出了 CAST，這是一個案例驅動的框架，它將歷史執行軌跡視為結構化案例。CAST 不是重複使用原始範例輸出，而是提取案例衍生訊號，以識別複雜性配置文件，用於估計最佳推理策略，以及失

primary_link.title: From Scenes to Elements: Multi-Granularity Evidence Retrieval for Verifiable Multimodal RAG

papers.summary: Multimodal Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems retrieve evidence at coarse granularities (entire images or scenes), creating a mismatch with fine-grained user queries and making failures unverifiable. We introduce GranuVistaVQA, a multimodal benchmark featuring real-world landmarks with element-level annotations across multiple viewpoints, capturing the partial observation challenge where individual images contain only subsets of entities. links.title: Chain-of-Procedure: Hierarchical Visual-Language Reasoning for Procedural QA

primary_link.title: Chain-of-Procedure: Hierarchical Visual-Language Reasoning for Procedural QA

papers.summary: Recent advances in vision-language models (VLMs) have achieved impressive results on standard image-text tasks, yet their potential for visual procedure question answering (VP-QA) remains largely unexplored. VP-QA presents unique challenges where users query next-step actions by uploading images for intermediate states of complex procedures. To systematically evaluate VLMs on this practical task, we propose ProcedureVQA, a novel multimodal benchmark specifically designed for visual procedural re
papers.summary_zh: 最近 vision-language models (VLMs) 在標準圖像-文本任務上取得了令人印象深刻的成果，但它們在 visual procedure question answering (VP-QA) 方面的潛力仍 largely unexplored。VP-QA 提出了獨特的挑戰，用戶透過上傳複雜程序的中間狀態圖像來查詢下一步操作。為了系統地評估 VLMs 在這項實用任務上的表現，我們提出了 ProcedureVQA，一個專為視覺程序性重 設計的新型多模態基準。

primary_link.title: Teaching Large Language Models When Not to Know: Learning Temporal Critique for Ex-Ante Reasoning

papers.summary: Large language models (LLMs) often fail to reason under temporal cutoffs: when prompted to answer from the standpoint of an earlier time, they exploit knowledge that became available only later. We study this failure through the lens of ex-ante reasoning, where a model must rely exclusively on information knowable before a cutoff. papers.summary_zh: 大型語言模型 (LLMs) 在 temporal cutoffs 下進行推理時經常失敗：當被要求從較早時間點的角度回答問題時，它們會利用僅在之後才可用的知識。我們透過 ex-ante reasoning 的視角研究了這種失敗，其中模型必須完全依賴在 cutoff 之前可知曉的資訊。透過對 prompt-level 干預的系統分析，我們發現 temporal leakage 對於 cutoff 的 formulation 和 instruction 的放置極為敏感：顯式
papers.title: Teaching Large Language Models When Not to Know: Learning Temporal Critique for Ex-Ante Reasoning
papers.title_zh: 教導大型語言模型何時不應知曉：學習用於 Ex-Ante Reasoning 的時間批判

links.title: Uncertainty Quantification for Large Language Diffusion Models

primary_link.title: Uncertainty Quantification for Large Language Diffusion Models

papers.summary: Large Language Diffusion Models (LLDMs) are emerging as an alternative to autoregressive models, offering faster inference through higher parallelism. Similar to autoregressive LLMs, they remain prone to hallucinations, making reliable uncertainty quantification (UQ) crucial for safe deployment. papers.summary_zh: 大型語言擴散模型 (LLDMs) 正在崛起，成為 autoregressive models 的替代方案，透過更高的 parallelism 提供更快的 inference 速度。與 autoregressive LLMs 類似，它們仍然容易產生 hallucinations，這使得可靠的 uncertainty quantification (UQ) 對於安全部署至關重要。然而，現有的 UQ 方法與這種新範式存在根本性的不匹配：它們假設 autoregressive factorization 或使用昂貴的重複採樣，從而抵消了 LLDMs 的效率。在本文中
papers.title: Uncertainty Quantification for Large Language Diffusion Models
papers.title_zh: 大型語言擴散模型的「不確定性量化」

links.title: TeachAnything: A Multimodal Crowdsourcing Platform for Training Embodied AI Agents in Symmetrical Reality

primary_link.title: TeachAnything: A Multimodal Crowdsourcing Platform for Training Embodied AI Agents in Symmetrical Reality

papers.summary: Symmetrical Reality (SR) is emerging as a future trend for human-agent coexistence, placing higher demands on agents to acquire human-like intelligence. It calls for richer and more diverse human guidance. We introduce a three-stage demonstration paradigm integrating multimodal demonstration signals. papers.summary_zh: Symmetrical Reality (SR) 正在成為人機共存的未來趨勢，對代理獲取類人智能提出了更高的要求。它需要更豐富和多樣化的人類指導。我們引入了一個整合多模態示範訊號的三階段示範範式。在此範式基礎上，我們開發了 TeachAnything，這是一個基於雲端、面向眾包的示範平台，具有 physics simulation 功能，能夠收集各種示範數據
papers.title: TeachAnything: A Multimodal Crowdsourcing Platform for Training Embodied AI Agents in Symmetrical Reality
papers.title_zh: TeachAnything：一個用於在「對稱現實」中訓練具身 AI 代理的多模態眾包平台

papers.title: Language Generation as Optimal Control: Closed-Loop Diffusion in Latent Control Space

links.title: Test-Time Learning with an Evolving Library

primary_link.title: Test-Time Learning with an Evolving Library

papers.summary: We introduce EvoLib, a test-time learning framework that enables large language models to accumulate, reuse, and evolve knowledge across problem instances without parameter updates or external supervision. Instead of adapting model parameters, our approach maintains a shared library of knowledge abstractions, including modular skills and reflective insights, automatically extracted from the model's own inference trajectories. To support continual improvement, we introduce a principled weighting
papers.summary_zh: 我們引入了 EvoLib，這是一個 test-time learning 框架，它使大型語言模型能夠在沒有 parameter updates 或 external supervision 的情況下，跨問題實例累積、重用和演進知識。我們的S方法不是調整模型參數，而是維護一個共享的知識抽象庫，包括從模型自身的 inference trajectories 中自動提取的 modular skills 和 reflective insights。為了支持持續改進，我們引入了一種有原則的加權
papers.title: Test-Time Learning with an Evolving Library
papers.title_zh: 帶有演進式知識庫的測試時間學習

links.title: MetaBackdoor: Exploiting Positional Encoding as a Backdoor Attack Surface in LLMs

primary_link.title: MetaBackdoor: Exploiting Positional Encoding as a Backdoor Attack Surface in LLMs

papers.summary: Backdoor attacks pose a serious security threat to large language models (LLMs), which are increasingly deployed as general-purpose assistants in safety- and privacy-critical applications. Existing LLM backdoors rely primarily on content-based triggers, requiring explicit modification of the input text. In this work, we show that this assumption is unnecessary and limiting. We introduce MetaBackdoor, a new class of backdoor attacks that exploits positional information as the trigger, without mod
papers.summary_zh: 後門攻擊對大型語言模型（LLMs）構成嚴重的安全威脅，LLMs 正越來越多地被部署為安全和隱私關鍵應用中的通用助手。現有的 LLM 後門主要依賴基於內容的觸發器，需要明確修改輸入文本。在這項工作中，我們表明這個假設是不必要且具有限制性的。我們引入 MetaBackdoor，這是一種新型的後門攻擊，它利用位置資訊作為觸發器，無需修改內容。
papers.title: MetaBackdoor: Exploiting Positional Encoding as a Backdoor Attack Surface in LLMs
papers.title_zh: MetaBackdoor: 利用位置編碼作為 LLMs 中的後門攻擊面

links.title: WARD: Adversarially Robust Defense of Web Agents Against Prompt Injections

primary_link.title: WARD: Adversarially Robust Defense of Web Agents Against Prompt Injections

papers.summary: Web agents can autonomously complete online tasks by interacting with websites, but their exposure to open web environments makes them vulnerable to prompt injection attacks embedded in HTML content or visual interfaces. Existing guard models still suffer from limited generalization to unseen domains and attack patterns, high false positive rates on benign content, reduced deployment efficiency due to added latency at each step, and vulnerability to adversarial attacks that evolve over time or d
papers.summary_zh: 網路代理可以透過與網站互動自主完成線上任務，但它們暴露在開放網路環境中，使其容易受到嵌入在 HTML 內容或視覺介面中的 Prompt Injection 攻擊。現有的防護模型仍然存在對未見過領域和攻擊模式泛化能力有限的問題，對良性內容的誤報率高，由於每一步都增加了延遲導致部署效率降低，以及容易受到隨著時間演變的對抗性攻擊的影響。
papers.title: WARD: Adversarially Robust Defense of Web Agents Against Prompt Injections
papers.title_zh: WARD: 針對 Prompt Injection 的網路代理對抗性穩健防禦

links.title: Toward Securing AI Agents Like Operating Systems

primary_link.title: Toward Securing AI Agents Like Operating Systems

papers.summary: Autonomous agents based on large language models (LLMs) are rapidly emerging as a general-purpose technology, with recent systems such as OpenClaw extending their capabilities through broad tool use, third-party skills, and deeper integration into user environments. At the same time, these agentic systems introduce substantial security risks by combining unconstrained capabilities with access to sensitive user data. In this work, we investigate the security of LLM-based agents through the lens o
papers.summary_zh: 基於大型語言模型（LLMs）的自主代理正迅速崛起成為一種通用技術，OpenClaw 等最新系統透過廣泛的工具使用、第三方技能以及更深層次地整合到使用者環境中，擴展了它們的能力。同時，這些代理系統透過將無限制的能力與敏感使用者資料的存取相結合，引入了巨大的安全風險。在這項工作中，我們透過作業系統的視角來調查基於 LLM 的代理的安全性。
papers.title: Toward Securing AI Agents Like Operating Systems
papers.title_zh: 邁向像作業系統一樣保護 AI 代理

links.title: One Step to the Side: Why Defenses Against Malicious Finetuning Fail Under Adaptive Adversaries

primary_link.title: One Step to the Side: Why Defenses Against Malicious Finetuning Fail Under Adaptive Adversaries

papers.summary: Model providers increasingly release open weights or allow users to fine-tune foundation models through APIs. Although these models are safety-aligned before release, their safeguards can often be removed by fine-tuning on harmful data. Recent defenses aim to make models robust to such malicious fine-tuning, but they are largely evaluated only against fixed attacks that do not account for the defense. We show that these robustness claims are incomplete. Surveying 15 recent defenses, we identify
papers.summary_zh: 模型供應商越來越多地發布開放權重或允許使用者透過 APIs 對基礎模型進行 Fine-tuning。儘管這些模型在發布前已進行安全對齊，但其安全措施通常可以透過對有害資料進行 Fine-tuning 來移除。最近的防禦措施旨在使模型對此類惡意 Fine-tuning 具有穩健性，但它們主要僅針對未考慮防禦的固定攻擊進行評估。我們證明這些穩健性聲明是不完整的。調查了 15 項最新防禦措施後，我們發現它們未能考慮到適應性攻擊。
papers.title: One Step to the Side: Why Defenses Against Malicious Finetuning Fail Under Adaptive Adversaries
papers.title_zh: 偏離一步：為何針對惡意 Fine-tuning 的防禦在適應性攻擊者下會失效

links.title: Talk is (Not) Cheap: A Taxonomy and Benchmark Coverage Audit for LLM Attacks

primary_link.title: Talk is (Not) Cheap: A Taxonomy and Benchmark Coverage Audit for LLM Attacks

papers.summary: We introduce a reusable framework for auditing whether LLM attack benchmarks collectively cover the threat surface: a 4$\times$6 Target $\times$ Technique matrix grounded in STRIDE, constructed from a 507-leaf taxonomy -- 401 data-populated and 106 threat-model-derived leaves -- of inference-time attacks extracted from 932 arXiv security studies (2023--2026). The matrix enables benchmark-external validation -- auditing collective coverage rather than individual benchmark consistency. Applying it
papers.summary_zh: 我們引入了一個可重複使用的框架，用於審計 LLM 攻擊基準是否共同覆蓋威脅面：這是一個基於 STRIDE 的 4x6 目標 × 技術矩陣，該矩陣從一個 507 個葉節點的分類法（其中 401 個為資料填充，106 個為威脅模型派生）構建，這些葉節點代表從 932 篇 arXiv 安全研究（2023-2026 年）中提取的推論時攻擊。該矩陣實現了基準外部驗證，即審計集體覆蓋範圍而非單個基準的一致性。應用它顯示出顯著的覆蓋範圍差距。
papers.title: Talk is (Not) Cheap: A Taxonomy and Benchmark Coverage Audit for LLM Attacks
papers.title_zh: 空談（不）廉價：LLM 攻擊的分類學和基準覆蓋審計

primary_link.title: Do Coding Agents Understand Least-Privilege Authorization?

links.title: Selective Safety Steering via Value-Filtered Decoding

primary_link.title: Selective Safety Steering via Value-Filtered Decoding

papers.summary: While large language models (LLMs) are trained to align with human values, their generations may still violate safety constraints. A growing line of work addresses this problem by modifying the model's sampling policy at decoding time using a safety reward. However, existing decoding-time steering methods often intervene unnecessarily, modifying generations that would have been safe under the base model. Such unnecessary interventions are undesirable, as they can distort key properties of the ba
papers.summary_zh: 儘管 Large Language Models (LLMs) 經過訓練以符合人類價值觀，但其生成內容仍可能違反安全限制。越來越多的研究透過在 decoding time 使用 safety reward 修改模型的 sampling policy 來解決這個問題。然而，現有的 decoding-time steering 方法經常不必要地進行干預，修改了在基礎模型下本來會是安全的生成內容。這種不必要的干預是不受歡迎的，因為它們會扭曲基礎模型的一些關鍵特性。
papers.title: Selective Safety Steering via Value-Filtered Decoding

primary_link.title: Defenses at Odds: Measuring and Explaining Defense Conflicts in Large Language Models

papers.summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) deployed in high-stakes applications must simultaneously manage multiple risks, yet existing defenses are almost exclusively evaluated in isolation under a one-shot deployment assumption. In practice, providers patch models incrementally throughout their lifecycle-responding to newly exposed vulnerabilities or targeted data-removal requests without retraining from scratch. papers.summary_zh: 部署在高風險應用中的 Large Language Models (LLMs) 必須同時管理多種風險，然而現有的防禦措施幾乎完全是在一次性部署假設下孤立地進行評估。實際上，供應商在其生命週期中會逐步修補模型，以應對新暴露的漏洞或有針對性的資料刪除請求，而無需從頭開始重新訓練。這提出了一個基本但尚未充分探討的問題：後來的防禦是否能保留原有的保護

papers.title_zh: 相互衝突的防禦：測量和解釋大型語言模型中的防禦衝突

These effects produce residual dynamics that are simultaneously cross-coupled, history-dependent, and nonstationary, causing both an
radar.summary_zh: 精確的動力學模型對於執行負載運輸等複雜任務的 aerial manipulators 至關重要。然而，由於強烈的 quadrotor-manipulator 耦合、延遲的空氣動力學交互作用以及因酬載變化和機械手重新配置而產生的 regime-dependent 動力學變異，對這些系統進行建模仍然面臨根本性挑戰。這些影響產生了同時具有 cross-coupled、history-dependent 和 nonstationary 特性的殘餘動力學，導致了
radar.title: Learning Cross-Coupled and Regime Dependent Dynamics for Aerial Manipulation

links.title: Adapting AlphaEvolve to Optimize Fully Homomorphic Encryption on TPUs

primary_link.title: Adapting AlphaEvolve to Optimize Fully Homomorphic Encryption on TPUs

radar.summary: The deployment of Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) at scale is hindered due to its heavy computational overhead. While specialized hardware accelerators like Google Tensor Processing Units (TPUs) can help, mapping complex cryptographic kernels onto such architectures remains a challenge. radar.summary_zh: Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) 的大規模部署因其龐大的計算開銷而受阻。雖然 Google Tensor Processing Units (TPUs) 等專用硬體加速器有所幫助，但將複雜的 cryptographic kernels 映射到此類架構上仍然是一個挑戰。高效率的執行需要 systolic array-based Matrix Multiplication Unit (MXU) 和 Vector Processing Units (VPUs) 之間的共同優化，以及跨越 vecto 的資料移動編排
radar.title: Adapting AlphaEvolve to Optimize Fully Homomorphic Encryption on TPUs
radar.title_zh: 調整 AlphaEvolve 以優化 TPU 上的 Fully Homomorphic Encryption

links.title: DSSP: Diffusion State Space Policy with Full-History Encoding

primary_link.title: DSSP: Diffusion State Space Policy with Full-History Encoding

radar.summary: Diffusion-based imitation learning has shown strong promise for robot manipulation. However, most existing policies condition only on the current observation or a short window of recent observations, limiting their ability to resolve history-dependent ambiguities in long-horizon tasks. Leveraging the continuous sequence modeling properti
radar.summary_zh: 基於 Diffusion 的 imitation learning 在機器人操作方面展現了巨大的潛力。然而，大多數現有 policies 僅基於當前觀察或短期歷史觀察進行條件設定，這限制了它們在長時程任務中解決 history-dependent 歧義的能力。為了應對此挑戰，我們引入了 DSSP，這是一種 history-conditioned Diffusion State Space Policy，它能為機器人操作實現高效的 full-history conditioning。利用連續序列建模的特性
radar.title: DSSP: Diffusion State Space Policy with Full-History Encoding
radar.title_zh: DSSP: 帶有全歷史編碼的 Diffusion State Space Policy

links.title: Let Robots Feel Your Touch: Visuo-Tactile Cortical Alignment for Embodied Mirror Resonance

primary_link.title: Let Robots Feel Your Touch: Visuo-Tactile Cortical Alignment for Embodied Mirror Resonance

radar.summary: Observing touch on another's body can elicit corresponding tactile sensations in the observer, a phenomenon termed mirror touch that supports empathy and social perception. This visuo-tactile resonance is thought to rely on structural correspondence between visual and somatosensory cortices, yet robotic systems lack computational frameworks that instantiate this principle. Here we demonstrate that cortical correspondence can be operationalized to endow robots with mirror touch. We introduce Mirr
radar.summary_zh: 觀察他人身體上的觸摸可以在觀察者中引起相應的觸覺，這種現象被稱為 mirror touch，它支持同理心和社會感知。這種 visuo-tactile resonance 被認為依賴於視覺和體感皮層之間的結構對應，然而機器人系統缺乏實現這一原則的計算框架。在此，我們證明 cortical correspondence 可以被操作化以賦予機器人 mirror touch。我們引入了 Mirr
radar.title: Let Robots Feel Your Touch: Visuo-Tactile Cortical Alignment for Embodied Mirror Resonance
radar.title_zh: 讓機器人感受你的觸摸：用於具身鏡像共振的視觸覺皮層對齊

links.title: A Prototyping Framework for Distributed Control of Multi-Robot Systems

primary_link.title: A Prototyping Framework for Distributed Control of Multi-Robot Systems

radar.summary: This paper presents a prototyping framework for distributed control of multi-robot systems, aimed at bridging theory and practical testing of distributed optimization algorithms. Using the Single Program, Multiple Data (SPMD) paradigm, the framework emulates distributed control on a single computer, with each core running the same algorithm using local states and neighbour-to-neighbour communication. We demonstrate the framework on a four-quadrotor position-swapping task using a non-cooperative
radar.summary_zh: 本文提出了一種用於多機器人系統分散式控制的原型框架，旨在彌合分散式優化演算法的理論與實際測試之間的鴻溝。該框架利用 Single Program, Multiple Data (SPMD) 範式，在單一電腦上模擬分散式控制，每個核心使用本地狀態和 neighbour-to-neighbour communication 運行相同的演算法。我們在一個使用 non-cooperative 的四旋翼位置交換任務上展示了該框架
radar.title: A Prototyping Framework for Distributed Control of Multi-Robot Systems
radar.title_zh: 一種用於多機器人系統分散式控制的原型框架

links.title: DiffPhD: A Unified Differentiable Solver for Projective Heterogeneous Materials in Elastodynamics with Contact-Rich GPU-Acceleration

primary_link.title: DiffPhD: A Unified Differentiable Solver for Projective Heterogeneous Materials in Elastodynamics with Contact-Rich GPU-Acceleration

radar.summary: Differentiable simulation of soft bodies is a foundation for system identification, trajectory optimization, and Real2Sim transfer. Yet, existing methods such as the differentiable Projective Dynamics (DiffPD) struggle when faced with heterogeneous materials with extreme stiffness contrasts, hyperelasticity under large deformations, and contact-rich interactions, which are common scenarios in the real world. We present DiffPhD, a unified GPU-accelerated differentiable Projective Dynamics framewo
radar.summary_zh: 軟體的可微分模擬是 system identification、trajectory optimization 和 Real2Sim transfer 的基礎。然而，現有方法，例如 differentiable Projective Dynamics (DiffPD)，在面對具有極端剛度對比的異質材料、大變形下的 hyperelasticity 以及 rich-contact 互動時，會遇到困難。這些都是現實世界中的常見場景。我們提出了 DiffPhD，一個統一的 GPU 加速 differentiable Projective Dynamics framework。
radar.title: DiffPhD: A Unified Differentiable Solver for Projective Heterogeneous Materials in Elastodynamics with Contact-Rich GPU-Acceleration
radar.title_zh: DiffPhD：一種用於彈性動力學中投影異質材料的統一可微分求解器，具有豐富接觸的 GPU 加速

links.title: H-OmniStereo: Zero-Shot Omnidirectional Stereo Matching with Heading-Aligned Normal Priors

primary_link.title: H-OmniStereo: Zero-Shot Omnidirectional Stereo Matching with Heading-Aligned Normal Priors

radar.summary: Stereo matching on top-bottom equirectangular images provides an effective framework for full-surround perception, as vertically aligned epipolar lines enable the use of advanced perspective stereo architectures that are largely driven by large-scale datasets and monocular priors. radar.summary_zh: 在頂部-底部 equirectangular 圖像上進行 Stereo matching 為 full-surround perception 提供了一個有效的 framework，因為垂直對齊的 epipolar lines 使得使用先進的 perspective stereo architectures 成為可能，而這些架構主要由大型 datasets 和 monocular priors 驅動。然而，這類改進的性能受到全向 stereo datasets 稀缺性以及 spherical distortions 下 perspective monocular priors 退化的嚴重限制。為了應對這些挑戰，我們提出了...
radar.title: H-OmniStereo: Zero-Shot Omnidirectional Stereo Matching with Heading-Aligned Normal Priors
radar.title_zh: H-OmniStereo：結合航向對齊法線先驗的 Zero-Shot 全向立體匹配

links.title: Analyzing Codes of Conduct for Online Safety in Video Games at Scale

primary_link.title: Analyzing Codes of Conduct for Online Safety in Video Games at Scale

radar.summary: Online video games have become major online social spaces where users interact, compete, and create together. These spaces, however, expose users to a wide spectrum of online harms, including harassment, discrimination, inappropriate content, privacy breach, cheating, and more. The shape and severity of such harms vary across game design, mechanics, and community context. To mitigate these harms, game companies issue Codes of Conduct (CoCs) that articulate online safety rules and direct players
radar.summary_zh: 線上電子遊戲已成為主要的線上社交空間，用戶在此互動、競爭並共同創造。然而，這些空間也讓用戶面臨廣泛的線上危害，包括騷擾、歧視、不當內容、隱私洩露、作弊等。這些危害的形式和嚴重程度因遊戲設計、機制和社群背景而異。為減輕這些危害，遊戲公司發布了行為準則 (CoCs)，闡明了線上安全規則並指導玩家。
radar.title: Analyzing Codes of Conduct for Online Safety in Video Games at Scale
radar.title_zh: 大規模分析電子遊戲線上安全的行為準則

links.title: SeaVis: Modeling and Control of a Remotely Operated Towed Vehicle for Seabed Visualization and Mapping

primary_link.title: SeaVis: Modeling and Control of a Remotely Operated Towed Vehicle for Seabed Visualization and Mapping

radar.summary: High-resolution seafloor mapping necessitates stable and precise positioning for underwater robots. This paper introduces a novel mathematical model for SeaVis remotely operated towed vehicles (ROTVs) and develops a gain-scheduled linear-quadratic regulator (LQR) for robust depth and attitude control. We validate the approach in a high-fidelity simulation, benchmarking the LQR against a conventional PID controller over a challenging seabed profile. The presented results demonstrate the LQR's sup
radar.summary_zh: 高解析度海床測繪需要水下機器人具備穩定且精確的定位。本文為 SeaVis remotely operated towed vehicles (ROTVs) 引入了一種新穎的數學模型，並開發了一個 gain-scheduled linear-quadratic regulator (LQR) 用於穩健的深度和姿態控制。我們在一個高擬真度模擬中驗證了該方法，並在一個具有挑戰性的海床剖面下，將 LQR 與傳統的 PID controller 進行了基準測試。所呈現的結果證明了 LQR 的卓越性能。
radar.title: SeaVis: Modeling and Control of a Remotely Operated Towed Vehicle for Seabed Visualization and Mapping
radar.title_zh: SeaVis：用於海床視覺化與測繪的遙控拖曳載具的建模與控制

links.title: Duality Between Chemical Potential Dynamics and Reaction-Diffusion Systems

primary_link.title: Duality Between Chemical Potential Dynamics and Reaction-Diffusion Systems

radar.summary: Pattern formation in soft, active, and biological matter is described by two ostensibly distinct continuum frameworks: phase-field theories driven by chemical-potential gradients, and mass-conserving reaction-diffusion (McRD) dynamics governed by local interconversion kinetics. radar.summary_zh: 軟物質、活性物質和生物物質中的圖案形成由兩個表面上不同的連續統 frameworks 描述：由 chemical-potential gradients 驅動的 phase-field theories，以及由局部 interconversion kinetics 控制的 mass-conserving reaction-diffusion (McRD) dynamics。在此，我們建立了一種在非線性、遠離平衡狀態下有效的建構性、方程級別的對偶性。McRD 是一個更廣泛的類別：每個具有 conserved order parameters 的 chemical-potential theory 都嵌入為慢動力學 (slow dynamics)。
radar.title: Duality Between Chemical Potential Dynamics and Reaction-Diffusion Systems
radar.title_zh: 化學勢動力學與反應-擴散系統之間的對偶性

primary_link.title: Behavioral Data-Driven Optimal Trajectory Generation for Rotary Cranes

radar.summary: With the growth of the construction industry and the global shortage of skilled labor, the automation of crane control has become increasingly important for safe and efficient operations. primary_link.title: Your CLIP has 164 dimensions of noise: Exploring the embeddings covariance eigenspectrum of contrastively pretrained vision-language transformers

top5.summary: Contrastively pre-trained Vision-Language Models (VLMs) serve as powerful feature extractors. Yet, their shared latent spaces are prone to structural anomalies and act as repositories for non-semantic, multi-modal noise. To address this phenomenon, we employ spectral decomposition of covariance matrices to decompose the VLM latent space into a multi-modal semantic signal component and a shared noise subspace. links.title: Pelican-Unified 1.0: A Unified Embodied Intelligence Model for Understanding, Reasoning, Imagination and Action

primary_link.title: Pelican-Unified 1.0: A Unified Embodied Intelligence Model for Understanding, Reasoning, Imagination and Action

top5.summary: We present Pelican-Unified 1.0, the first embodied foundation model trained according to the principle of unification. Pelican-Unified 1.0 uses a single VLM as a unified understanding module, mapping scenes, instructions, visual contexts, and action histories into a shared semantic space. links.title: EVA: Editing for Versatile Alignment against Jailbreaks

primary_link.title: EVA: Editing for Versatile Alignment against Jailbreaks

top5.summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) and Vision Language Models (VLMs) have demonstrated impressive capabilities but remain vulnerable to jailbreaking attacks, where adversaries exploit textual or visual triggers to bypass safety guardrails. Recent defenses typically rely on safety fine-tuning or external filters to reduce the model's likelihood of producing harmful content. top5.summary_zh: Large Language Models (LLMs) 和 Vision Language Models (VLMs) 展現了令人印象深刻的能力，但仍然容易受到 jailbreaking 攻擊，攻擊者利用文本或視覺觸發器來繞過安全防護措施。最近的防禦措施通常依賴於 safety fine-tuning 或外部過濾器來降低模型生成有害內容的可能性。雖然在一定程度上有效，但這些方法通常會產生顯著的計算開銷，並且存在 safety utility t

links.title: MemLens: Benchmarking Multimodal Long-Term Memory in Large Vision-Language Models

primary_link.title: MemLens: Benchmarking Multimodal Long-Term Memory in Large Vision-Language Models

top5.summary: Memory is essential for large vision-language models (LVLMs) to handle long, multimodal interactions, with two method directions providing this capability: long-context LVLMs and memory-augmented agents. However, no existing benchmark conducts a systematic comparison of the two on questions that genuinely require multimodal evidence. To close this gap, we introduce MEMLENS, a comprehensive benchmark for memory in multimodal multi-session conversations, comprising 789 questions across five memory
top5.summary_zh: 記憶對於大型視覺語言模型 (LVLMs) 處理長期、多模態互動至關重要，有兩個方法方向提供此能力：long-context LVLMs 和 memory-augmented agents。然而，現有 benchmark 都未能針對真正需要多模態證據的問題，對這兩者進行系統性的比較。為了彌補這一空白，我們引入了 MEMLENS，一個用於多模態多會話對話記憶的綜合 benchmark，包含五種記憶類型的 789 個問題
top5.title: MemLens: Benchmarking Multimodal Long-Term Memory in Large Vision-Language Models
